content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory architectures can be re-engineered to store and prioritize the "novelty prediction errors" that drive curiosity in intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) autonomous agents, creating a self-improving curiosity cycle where the agent actively seeks out unlearned environments aligned with its stored unresolvable novelty patterns. This design eliminates the common problem of curiosity-driven agents getting stuck in noisy, high-novelty but low-learning-value traps by leveraging structured memory retrieval to bias exploration toward high-impact unlabeled data.
